trade.summary: Limited bilateral trade. Poland exports machinery, chemicals, and furniture; Mexico exports vehicles, electronics, and fresh produce. Trade is largely channeled through broader EU-Mexico frameworks.

diplomatic.summary: Normal diplomatic relations. Both countries cooperate within multilateral organizations. Poland's EU membership frames much of the relationship. Limited direct bilateral political engagement beyond standard embassy functions.

military.summary: No bilateral military relationship. Poland is a NATO member with growing defense commitments; Mexico is non-aligned.

financial.summary: Very limited direct investment flows. Some Polish companies have indirect exposure to Mexico through EU-based multinational operations.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. Both countries are seeking to diversify their energy mixes but through different pathways.

scientific.summary: Modest academic cooperation, primarily through EU-Latin America research programs. Some exchanges in chemistry and engineering fields.
